title: mini status --phase <n>
goal: mini status --phase <n> prints a detailed view of one phase: title, goal, status, duration, every step with its detail and status, and the phase run report (verdict, verify items, free-text body); an unknown <n> fails with a clean error. Works with --json too (the single phase, with run report body). Verifiable via tests.

steps.title: Pure renderer renderPhaseDetail
steps.detail: Pure function in status.ts that turns a phase (+ optional run report) into detail lines: title/goal/status/duration, every step with its detail and status, and the run report (verdict, verify items, free-text body). TTY-free, covered by a unit test.

steps.title: status --phase <n>: human view
steps.detail: Add --phase <n> to StatusOptions + cli.ts; it looks the phase up (unknown <n> → clean error, exit 1), reads its run report and prints via the renderer instead of the whole overview. Test in status.test.ts.

steps.title: --json --phase <n>: single-phase detail
steps.detail: When both flags are set, output JSON for just that one phase — steps including their detail, and the run report (verdict, verify, body). Dedicated builder, covered by a test.

steps.title: Docs
steps.detail: Document --phase in docs/non-interactive/status.md (both the human and --json modes).
